title: Basic
summary: Defines the formal Poincaré–Einstein space, its bulk and boundary dimensions, compactification data, and Einstein condition.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines the formal Poincaré–Einstein space, its bulk and boundary dimensions, compactification data, and Einstein condition.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Basic.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Topological and dimensional data for a conformally compact bulk. Its mathematical data are: bulk: Type u; boundary: Type v; bulk Dim: Nat; boundary Dim: Nat; dim relation: bulk Dim equals boundary Dim plus 1; boundary Inclusion: boundary maps to bulk; interior: Set bulk; boundary not interior: for every x : boundary, boundary Inclusion x ∉ interior.
For X of type PESpace, X.bulk Dim equals X.boundary Dim plus 1.
A scalar function on a formal geometric carrier. In explicit terms, for x a type, its defining expression is X maps to Real.

Formal metric expressions. Only the operations needed by conformal compactification, PE normal form, and the cone decomposition are included at this stage. Its constructors are: zero with arguments : Metric Symbol X; atom with arguments : Nat maps to Metric Symbol X; add with arguments : Metric Symbol X maps to Metric Symbol X maps to Metric Symbol X; scalar SMul with arguments : Real maps to Metric Symbol X maps to Metric Symbol X; function SMul with arguments : Function Symbol X maps to Metric Symbol X maps to Metric Symbol X.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{MetricSymbol}::=\operatorname{zero}(\colon \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X)\mid\operatorname{atom}(\colon \mathbb{N} \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X)\mid\operatorname{add}(\colon \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X)\mid\operatorname{scalarSMul}(\colon \mathbb{R} \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X)\mid\operatorname{functionSMul}(\colon \operatorname{FunctionSymbol} X \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Multiplication of a metric expression by a scalar function. In explicit terms, for a of type function symbol x, and g of type metric symbol x, its defining expression is Metric Symbol.function SMul a g.
correspondence.leanType: def scaleByFunction (a : FunctionSymbol X) (g : MetricSymbol X) : MetricSymbol X

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Multiplication of a metric expression by a real scalar. In explicit terms, for a in the real numbers, and g of type metric symbol x, its defining expression is Metric Symbol.scalar SMul a g.
correspondence.leanType: def scaleByScalar (a : Real) (g : MetricSymbol X) : MetricSymbol X

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The conformal structure induced at infinity. Its mathematical data are: boundary Conf: Conformal Structure. v, w X.boundary.

Abstract bulk metric data. The Ricci tensor is supplied as a symbolic metric-type tensor. The Einstein equation is imposed in EinsteinEquation.lean. Its mathematical data are: conformal Infinity: Conformal Infinity. u, v, w X; g Plus: Metric Symbol X.bulk; ricci: Metric Symbol X.bulk.

The symbolic right-hand side -n gPlus of the PE Einstein equation. In explicit terms, for x of type pespace, and gplus of type metric symbol x.bulk, its defining expression is Metric Symbol.scale By Scalar -X.boundary Dim g Plus.
correspondence.leanType: def einsteinRHS (X : PESpace) (gPlus : MetricSymbol X.bulk) : MetricSymbol X.bulk

references.note: Source for Poincaré normal form and its relation to straight ambient metrics.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
